overview: Temur Kona leverages land acceleration and spell discounting to deploy large threats ahead of curve. By combining Formidable Speaker's mana generation with Omniscience's zero-cost casting, the deck overwhelms opponents through rapid board presence and value. The strategy focuses on maintaining tempo while setting up North Wind Avatar as a resilient finisher.
win_conditions: Casting North Wind Avatar for free during Omniscience
Overwhelming the board with multiple Formidable Speakers
Generating excess mana to cast multiple threats in a single turn

keep_criteria: Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritizing fetches/shocks if in format), at least one early spell or ramp piece, and a clear path to casting Formidable Speaker or Omniscience by turn 3-4.
mulligan_criteria: Three or more lands without ramp
High mana cost threats without acceleration
Mulligan if you have zero lands, only late-game threats without enablers, or a hand entirely composed of high-cost spells with no early interaction.

early_game: Prioritize land drops and Formidable Speaker activation
Hold up mana for potential interaction
Avoid committing to North Wind Avatar prematurely
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cheap cantrip or ramp spell to dig for your enablers.
Turn 2: Develop mana base, cast additional spells to build synergy counters/triggers, and set up for Omniscience.
Land sequencing: Prioritize sources that enter untapped or fetch lands to smooth draws
mid_game: Cast Omniscience once you have excess mana
Deploy multiple threats and interaction for free
Use Speaker's draw ability to find answers
Hoard low-cost spells to fuel spell triggers and ensure you have enough resources to cast Omniscience and follow-up threats.
Manage mana carefully to avoid flooding
use cantrips to dig for answers.
Treat the graveyard as a secondary resource for recursion or spell synergy, but protect it from hate if the format rewards it.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to resolution or can protect them with counterspells/removal.
Hold back high-value spells if the board is unstable or if you're facing a hard counter to your game plan.
late_game: Leverage mana advantage to outvalue opponents
Protect key permanents from board wipes
North Wind Avatar (high power/flying finisher) and Formidable Speaker (spell-synergy enabler/finisher).
Win by casting these with Omniscience or building enough spell triggers to overwhelm the board.
Use cantrips to dig for the final piece when resources are low.
Go all-in when Omniscience is online, you have sufficient mana for a turn, and the board is clear or you can protect your finisher.
Commit when you can resolve your threat through the stack or when the opponent has no interaction left.

key_cards.name: Formidable Speaker
key_cards.notes: Activate early to enable Omniscience and draw into threats

key_cards.name: Omniscience
key_cards.notes: Cast with excess mana to deploy multiple threats and finish the game

key_cards.name: North Wind Avatar
key_cards.notes: Save for Omniscience turns or use as a resilient blocker

sequencing_tips: Hold up blue and green mana for potential interaction before committing to Omniscience
Activate Formidable Speaker before casting Omniscience to maximize free spells
Prioritize casting North Wind Avatar last during Omniscience to avoid early removal
Common mistakes include overcommitting early without Omniscience, mismanaging mana for the enabler, and ignoring graveyard hate.
Unique interactions: Omniscience combined with spell triggers can create massive value, but be wary of format-specific rules about spell stacking.
Practice stack management and learn to read opponent's mana holdings to optimize your plays.

matchups.advice: Play defensively early, use Speaker to ramp past their curve, and save Omniscience for a turn where you can deploy a large threat and clear their board.
Race their hand size and removal by going wide with cheap threats, then close out with North Wind Avatar.
Match their resource output with Speaker, then use Omniscience to swing the tempo advantage decisively.

Use spell-slow, split the stack by casting multiple spells in response to counters, or leverage Omniscience to cast around them. Prioritize cantrips to dig through counters and maintain card advantage.
Apply early pressure and use spot removal on key ramp creatures. Disrupt their mana development with discard or early interaction to prevent them from out-accelerating you.
Value card advantage and protect your enablers. Out-resolve their spells with counterspells or spell-slow, and prioritize graveyard/hand disruption to disrupt their combo.

common_threats: Watch for Izzet's counterspells and stack manipulation
note when they hold up mana for removal versus counters.
Track landfall triggers on Selesnya/Mono-Green and anticipate their big turns.
Pay attention to graveyard activity and hand size to gauge their combo potential.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Formidable Speaker, Omniscience, North Wind Avatar

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Omniscience enables free casting of large threats

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard tempo sequencing principles applied to U/R/G ramp

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard format meta expectations for U/R/G tempo
